title: Stones of Color
editorialSummary: Gemstones grouped by the color they are most commonly found in. False trail: Lapis Lazuli and Sapphire are both blue but differ entirely — Sapphire is corundum, Lapis Lazuli is a rock composed of several minerals. Garnet is most commonly red but exists in green and other varieties — placed in Red as its canonical color. Sugilite is always purple and relatively rare. Green Tourmaline shares green color with Jade and Malachite but is a distinct silicate mineral. Items in puzzle-0355 (hard-024) are fully excluded to prevent cross-puzzle overlap.
